synthesis.summary: Coinbase received CFTC regulatory approval to offer crypto futures trading in the US, directly expanding its addressable product set and revenue potential in a market it was previously blocked from. The green light removes a key regulatory overhang and opens a new business line, justifying a meaningful single-day price jump. Volume was below average (rel_volume 0.69), suggesting the move was price-driven rather than broad participation, but the news mechanism is clear.

Dell reported blowout AI server/infrastructure sales in its latest earnings, directly driving the 30%+ single-day surge on more than double normal volume. Multiple analysts followed by arguing the AI-driven revenue acceleration has further room to run, adding to the momentum.

NTAP surged ~27% today on 3.5x normal volume, hitting an RSI of 92 — an extreme move with no direct company-specific news in the supplied set. The only related item is a market overview noting Dell jumped 28% on blowout AI server sales; NTAP likely caught a powerful halo effect as investors rotated into enterprise storage and AI infrastructure peers, but no NTAP-specific catalyst is present to explain the full magnitude.

ASTS dropped 17% on elevated volume with no high-impact company-specific news to explain the move. The only supplied headline vaguely references a "Rocket Explosion Is Bad News For Space Mania," which could have dampened sentiment across space-related names, but no direct catalyst for ASTS is present — this looks like sector-wide de-risking or options/flow-driven selling rather than a fundamental development.

CCCC surged 19% today on 1.71x relative volume with no identifiable news catalyst. The only news item is a generic sector roundup with low impact that lists the stock as a mover but does not explain what drove the move — likely options flow, short covering, or undisclosed institutional activity.

Firefly Aerospace priced a public offering at $48 per share, a discount to the pre-announcement price, creating immediate dilution pressure that drove the stock down roughly 7.7%. The elevated volume (2.6x normal) is consistent with the typical selling and arbitrage activity that follows a discounted equity raise.

Gap reported mixed Q1 results and issued weak forward guidance, directly triggering the ~18% single-day selloff on more than double normal volume. The combination of a below-expectation earnings print and a disappointing outlook gave investors concrete reasons to reprice the stock sharply lower.

OKTA surged 29% on unusually high volume, a move of this magnitude almost certainly driven by a material earnings beat, raised guidance, or major business development — but the only supplied news is a Benzinga analyst note reacting to a 52-week high, which is descriptive rather than explanatory. The actual catalyst (likely an earnings or guidance event given the size and volume spike) is not present in the provided news items.

PD surged 32.55% on dramatically elevated volume (2.66x normal), but the only supplied news is a generic sector roundup with no company-specific catalyst. A move of this magnitude — pushing RSI to 80.8 — almost certainly reflects a material company event such as an earnings beat, acquisition, or strategic announcement that is not present in the provided news.

PURR is up ~17.5% on nearly double its average volume, but the only available news — a general crypto industry comment from Intercontinental Exchange — has no direct connection to PURR or its business. The move is unexplained by supplied news and likely driven by unobservable factors such as options flow, retail momentum, or broader crypto-adjacent speculation.

REPL surged 83% on the day with volume nearly 4x normal, but the only supplied news is a generic multi-stock roundup with no detail on a company-specific catalyst. A move of this magnitude almost certainly reflects a discrete binary event — such as a clinical trial readout, FDA decision, or M&A activity — that is not captured in the provided news.

SPCE is up 32% on the day and 118% over five days with volume nearly 5x normal and RSI deep into overbought territory, but none of the supplied news items provide any fundamental catalyst — both are generic sector roundups with low impact. The move looks like a retail-driven momentum squeeze or options flow event with no identifiable news trigger.

INTC fell 2.3% on well-below-average volume (0.45x relative), with no company-specific news in the supplied items to explain the drop. The available headlines are low-impact, general sector commentary with no direct INTC catalyst, suggesting this is likely routine profit-taking, sector rotation, or broader market flows.
